mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient intercepts the single-byte color_type equality, preserving inputs whose byte at offset 0x19 moves toward the constant 3, while cmplog (I2S present but no CMP_MAP) gets no gradient.
one_line: VP gradient walks single color_type byte toward PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient rewards inputs progressively closer to 'xml:id' prefix+localname byte comparisons, letting the corpus climb toward the exact xml:id token that plain cmplog cannot ratchet toward without gradient feedback.
one_line: VP gradient climbs progressively toward xml:id attribute token

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards inputs whose attribute-type token bytes approach the 2-byte 'ID' literal between longer ID-prefixed keyword alternatives, giving graded credit that cmplog's edge-only signal lacks.
one_line: VP gradient steers attribute-type token to exact 2-byte 'ID' keyword

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient rewards partial progress on chained byte comparisons gating the DTD content-model grammar, letting VPC assemble the depth needed for the `last` pointer to become non-NULL and trigger the error arm.
one_line: VP gradient builds DTD content-model depth to set last pointer non-NULL

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient (added by vpc over cmplog's I2S) rewards partial matches on multi-byte literal comparisons along the DTD content-model path, enabling construction of a two-child content group that cmplog cannot assemble.
one_line: VP gradient builds multi-child DTD content group so both cur and last are non-NULL

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets give partial-match credit on the chained three-character 'ANY' equality that cmplog cannot reward; only the gradient carries the corpus across all three positions simultaneously.
one_line: VP gradient resolves three-byte 'ANY' keyword gate that cmplog cannot climb

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient rewards byte-by-byte progress toward the NXT(2)=='N' dispatch and downstream NOTATION keyword comparisons, steering the corpus toward the '<!N' token that cmplog's edge-only feedback never accumulates toward.
one_line: VP gradient climbs toward 'N' switch arm for NOTATION declaration token

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient buckets give partial-credit feedback for the RAW vs ';' comparison region, preserving near-miss seeds inching toward the 0x3b terminator that cmplog's edge-only signal cannot bank progress toward.
one_line: VP gradient incrementally banks progress toward ';' PE-reference terminator

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient gives graded credit on each byte-comparison step toward the 5-byte 'xmlns' literal, letting VPC incrementally intern the attribute name to the ctxt->str_xmlns pointer that cmplog's all-or-nothing feedback never reaches.
one_line: VP gradient incrementally interns attribute name to 5-byte 'xmlns' pointer

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient accumulates partial-match credit on the DOCTYPE/internal-subset structural comparisons, steering the corpus toward an inline DTD subset where inSubset==1 fires, which cmplog's edge+I2S stack alone cannot achieve.
one_line: VP gradient steers corpus toward inline internal DTD subset for inSubset==1

mechanism_summary: The blocker is a pointer-identity compare (prefix==pref) whose operands are runtime-interned heap pointers; I2S cannot substitute across it, so only CMP_MAP gradient on surrounding string/dict comparisons guides VPC to build the correctly-bound prefix namespace structure.
one_line: VP gradient on surrounding dict comparisons drives prefix/pref pointer identity

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient on prefix/URI string comparisons gives VPC partial-credit toward valid namespaced markup where URI!=NULL and ret->ns==NULL, climbing the namespace-resolution path that cmplog's all-or-nothing signal cannot bridge.
one_line: VP gradient climbs namespace-resolution path to URI!=NULL and ret->ns==NULL gate

mechanism_summary: Value profile's CMP_MAP buckets reward partial Hamming/prefix progress on the chained byte comparisons for the '0x' prefix, guiding the corpus to assemble the multi-byte hex token that pure cmplog edge feedback cannot incrementally reward.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient assembles '0x' hex prefix against single-byte x/X gate

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient buckets reward partial Hamming/prefix progress on the chained byte comparisons for z[0]=='0', z[1]=='x'/'X', isxdigit(z[2]), guiding the corpus to assemble the '0x' hex-token prefix that pure cmplog edge feedback cannot incrementally reward.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient assembles '0x' hex-token prefix against chained byte equality checks
